subject.name: Ibrahim Ibrahim
subject.bio: I've no idea what I am but I definitely know what I want

claims.claim_text: Writes production-quality Rust across very different domains — a Redis-like TCP server with concurrent clients and key expiration, plus low-level Solana programs in the Pinocchio framework.

evidence.excerpt: A minimal Redis-like TCP server built in Rust. Supports concurrent clients, key expiration, and structured logging.
P.O.S.T is short for Program-Owned SOL Treasury. It's a treasury factory — Rust

claims.claim_text: Has shipped real, deployed Solana programs end-to-end — including a tipping dApp with a live frontend, on-chain program ID, and PDA-based user profiles.

evidence.excerpt: Deployed Frontend URL: https://euergetes.vercel.app · Solana Program ID: EjYxegxKpBLxr1cZfbVzkGKWdD31F4w23kd5x2AMeB11
A decentralized instant tipping application built on Solana that enables users to send tips directly to content creators, service providers, or anyone with a Solana wallet.

claims.claim_text: Has implemented the canonical primitives of on-chain finance — escrows, vaults, flashloans, and a fundraiser — in the Pinocchio framework.
Reaches for the right concurrency primitives without flinching — built a multi-client TCP server using Rust's ownership model, std::thread, and Arc<Mutex<T>>.

evidence.excerpt: Concurrent clients via std::thread + Arc<Mutex<Database>>. Lazy expiration — expired keys are evicted on GET. Structured logging via the tracing crate.

claims.claim_text: Treats unfamiliar territory as a build target. Picks a meaty topic — Rust ownership, threading, TCP — and ships a working artefact at the end of the week.

evidence.excerpt: Built as a 3-day learning project to explore Rust concepts including ownership, threading, Arc<Mutex<T>>, and TCP networking.

claims.claim_text: Documents projects in the way good open-source maintainers do — explicit deployment URLs, program IDs, architecture sections, PDA seed conventions, and a usage walkthrough.

evidence.excerpt: PDAs Used: Tipper Stats PDA — Derived from seeds ["tipper_stats", tipper_wallet_pubkey] — stores tipping statistics for each user including total tips sent, total amount tipped, biggest tip, and last tip timestamp.

claims.claim_text: Bridges low-level Solana programs to TypeScript clients — escrow + scheduler integrations using Tuktuk, plus a Solana GPT escrow.

evidence.excerpt: Improved escrow-litesvm example but with TukTuk for scheduler and cron — TypeScript
Escrow program using Tuktuk and Solana GPT — TypeScript
